sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच
अयतिः श्रद्धयोपेतो योगाच्चलितमानसः।
अप्राप्य योगसंसिद्धिं कां गतिं कृष्ण गच्छति॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
ayatiḥ śraddhayopeto yogāc calita-mānasaḥ
aprāpya yoga-saṁsiddhiṁ kāṁ gatiṁ kṛṣṇa gacchati
literal_meaning: arjunaḥ uvāca—Arjuna said; ayatiḥ—unsuccessful; śraddhayā—with faith; upetaḥ—endowed; yogāt—from yoga; calita—deviated; mānasaḥ—mind; aprāpya—not attaining; yoga-saṁsiddhim—perfection in yoga; kām—which; gatim—destination; kṛṣṇa—O Kṛṣṇa; gacchati—goes
contextual_meaning: Arjuna voices the fear that many earnest seekers share: “If I begin a transformative practice with sincerity but later lose momentum and fall short, what becomes of me? Do I lose everything, or is there residual benefit?”
psychological_state: Performance anxiety masked as spiritual inquiry; anticipatory grief over possible self-failure.
trigger_scenario: Someone starts therapy or a fitness regime with enthusiasm, relapses into old habits, and panics that all prior effort was wasted.
anchor_text: Even a broken effort leaves lasting strength; no sincere step is ever lost.
